review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: My 1 year experience at Ipru MMS grad, off campus recruitment
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Biggest pro is that the salary is always credited on the last day on the month and almost never delayed. They also pay everyone's phone bills. Work hours are also not long, if you finish your work soon then you may leave, ( depends on your reporting manager tho )
Work pressure is not too crazy
PSF channel (department in sales) which is where I am makes sense only if you're someone can source business in high ticket size which will be the case if you have decent industry experience and good existing client base only then you can earn incentive.
For people new to Industry should go for "Banca" channel
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
Ipru offers one of the least pay of many other large insurance companies out there.
HR's are not super supportive nor they entertain any negotiation regarding any aspect.
Growth is extremely slow in this org, I've seen people working 12-15 year but still not on a meaningfully higher position.
The incentive structure has way too many criteria's for one to qualify for any decent incentive.
They offer very low salary if you don't have insurance experience or being recruited outside campus

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: I was working in this company a long time ago but had such a horrific experience that i promised myself i will do this some day. No Pros as far as i can remember
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
When I was working there, and i know this a long time ago in 2006 the company was horrendous in the way it treated employees. the company culture, company ethos, approach to work, the way employees were treated was shocking. 

Although i was working in Customer Service, sales was a major part of what we did as a company. There were sales managers that would use foul and abusive language almost at the rate of one swear word per minute in their sentences when talking to their teams just because a sales target was not met. Policy holders were given no respect as the were just means of achieving targets and the only objective it seemed was to increase the target at any cost.  

Employees are treated more as slaves and less as employees. We were made to work 7 days a week for the month of march without any leave of monetary compensation. There was no choice given to employees weather they want to work for 7 days or not it was IMPOSED! I am not sure how that equates to ethical employee welfare policy. 

Working there had no work life balance what so every, it was pretty much a very dull and grim place to work with chronic lack for imagination, creativity and work force well being. 

Please be advice that this only reflects my personal opinion, since I was working there I believe I am entitled to one.
